User Story: As a user I add a git repo so I can browse its worktrees/changes/files.
Expected Behavior: Choose a directory; if it answers `git worktree list` it is added, persisted, selected, and its primary worktree loads. Reachable from empty-state button, WORKTREES + button, and ··· menu.

User Story: As a user I am told when a chosen folder is not a git repo.
Expected Behavior: Adding a non-repo directory shows an error, adds nothing, repositories unchanged.

User Story: As a user adding the same repo twice does not create duplicates.
Expected Behavior: Path is canonicalized (tilde+realpath); a repo already present is not re-added (returns false).

User Story: As a user I remove a repo I no longer track.
Expected Behavior: ··· > Remove <name> deletes it, clears selection if it was active, and persists.

User Story: As a user I switch between added repos.
Expected Behavior: ··· menu lists repos; selecting one loads its worktrees + branches and focuses primary worktree.

User Story: As a user I set a repo's base branch for ahead/behind + 'about to ship'.
Expected Behavior: ··· > Set Base Branch > <local branch> stores baseBranch and persists it.
Errors: Set Base Branch persists repo.baseBranch but nothing consumes it: no diff-vs-base / ahead-of-base / about-to-ship view is wired. Setting it has no observable effect.
Fix: PENDING: wire an 'about to ship' diff-vs-base view (or remove the dead control).

User Story: As a user I create a new linked worktree on a new branch.
Expected Behavior: ··· > New Worktree… opens a save panel; creates `worktree add -b <folder>` and reselects the repo so the new worktree appears.

User Story: As a user I remove a linked worktree.
Expected Behavior: Row context menu > Remove Worktree… (non-primary only) runs `worktree remove` (force:false) and reselects repo.
Errors: removeWorktree force is hardcoded false; on a dirty/locked worktree git fails and AppModel dumps the raw GitError via "\(error)" into a 1-line truncated red label. No readable message, no force/confirm option.
Fix: PENDING: surface WorktreeModel.describe(error); optionally offer Force remove.

User Story: As a user I reveal a worktree directory in Finder.
Expected Behavior: Row menu > Open in Finder reveals the path.

User Story: As a user I open a worktree in Terminal.
Expected Behavior: Row menu > Open in Terminal launches Terminal.app at the path.

User Story: As a user I cannot remove the repo's primary checkout by mistake.
Expected Behavior: Remove Worktree… is hidden for the primary worktree.

User Story: As a user I see all worktrees of the selected repo.
Expected Behavior: Worktrees listed primary-first, then case-insensitive by name.

User Story: As a user I pick a worktree to inspect.
Expected Behavior: Tapping a row highlights it and loads its file tree + status into CHANGES/FILES.

User Story: As a user I see each worktree's divergence at a glance.
Expected Behavior: Each row shows `↓behind ↑ahead` from status.

User Story: As a user I see which worktree an agent is actively writing.
Expected Behavior: A green dot pulses when the worktree was written within the busy window (5s).
Errors: LiveDot pulse animation only starts in .onAppear behind guard active; no .onChange(of:active), so it never animates when isLive flips true (renders a static dot). Also isLive is recomputed only on selectRepo + manual refresh, never on FSEvents, so it rarely reads live at all.
Fix: PENDING: animate on active change; recompute worktree info on file-watch activity.

User Story: As a user I manually refresh sync/live state.
Expected Behavior: The refresh button re-queries ahead/behind/change-count/live for all worktrees.

User Story: As a user a collapsed WORKTREES header still shows the active worktree.
Expected Behavior: Collapsed header shows the active branch name + live dot.

User Story: As a user I get guidance when there is nothing to show.
Expected Behavior: Shows 'No repository added' or 'No worktrees' appropriately.

User Story: As a user I see working changes grouped by folder.
Expected Behavior: Changes group by parent folder, sorted; each shows filename + status letter.

User Story: As a user I see how many files changed.
Expected Behavior: Header shows a count badge equal to changes.count.

User Story: As a user I stage a file.
Expected Behavior: Context menu Stage runs `git add` via the serial queue and refreshes.

User Story: As a user I unstage a staged file.
Expected Behavior: Context menu Unstage runs `git restore --staged` and refreshes.

User Story: As a user I discard working changes with a safety confirm.
Expected Behavior: Discard… opens a confirm dialog; on confirm runs `git restore` and refreshes.

User Story: As a user I remove an untracked file safely.
Expected Behavior: For an untracked file Discard… runs `git clean -f` (not restore) after confirm.

User Story: As a user I commit my changes with a message.
Expected Behavior: Typing a message + Commit button or ⌘↩/Enter stages everything not staged then commits and clears the message.
Errors: Commit box placeholder advertises '⌘↩ to commit' but no keyboardShortcut is bound; the multiline .onSubmit is unreliable. Commit works only via the button.  Also: commit drops the unstaged half of a partially-staged file (stages only !isStaged paths).
Fix: PENDING: add .keyboardShortcut(.return, modifiers: .command) to the Commit button.

User Story: As a user the commit affordance reflects whether I can commit.
Expected Behavior: Button disabled with 'Nothing to commit' (0 changes) or 'Enter a message…' (empty); else 'Commit N change(s)'.

User Story: As a user I preview a change's diff with space.
Expected Behavior: Selecting a change then space opens the floating diff peek (staged or unstaged diff as appropriate).

User Story: As a user the open peek updates as I pick another change.
Expected Behavior: While the peek is open, selecting another change live-updates its content.

User Story: As a user I browse the worktree's files as a tree.
Expected Behavior: Directories first then files; subdirectories load lazily on expand.

User Story: As a user I open and close folders.
Expected Behavior: Tapping the triangle/row, double-click, or Enter toggles a directory; children appear indented.

User Story: As a user I see which files changed within the tree.
Expected Behavior: Changed files show a status letter; directories containing changes show an amber dot.

User Story: As a user I filter the tree by filename.
Expected Behavior: Typing a query yields a flat list of matching files.
Errors: File search only matches files in already-expanded/loaded subtrees (no recursive disk scan); files in collapsed folders are silently missed. Documented limitation.
Fix: Accepted limitation (documented); optional: hint 'searches expanded folders'.

User Story: As a user I sort files by name or recently changed.
Expected Behavior: FILES ··· > Sort toggles Name vs Recently changed (by mtime, dirs first).

User Story: As a user I optionally reveal git-ignored files.
Expected Behavior: FILES ··· > Show ignored rebuilds the tree including ignored paths.

User Story: As a user I narrow the tree to only changed files.
Expected Behavior: PRD §5.1 All|Changed: model.filter=.changed should derive the tree from the change list.
Errors: All|Changed filter logic exists and is unit-tested, but NO UI control sets worktree.filter, so the tree is permanently stuck on .all.
Fix: PENDING: add an All|Changed Picker bound to $worktree.filter in the FILES ··· menu.

User Story: As a user I move the selection with arrow keys.
Expected Behavior: Up/Down move selection through visible rows, clamped at ends.

User Story: As a user Enter opens a file or toggles a folder.
Expected Behavior: Enter on a file opens it in its native app; on a folder toggles expansion.

User Story: As a user a collapsed FILES header shows the active branch.
Expected Behavior: Collapsed FILES header shows the active worktree's branch/name.

User Story: As a user I get guidance when there are no files.
Expected Behavior: Shows 'No files' or 'Add a repository to get started'.

User Story: As a user I open a file in its native app.
Expected Behavior: Open (menu/Enter/double-click) launches the default app; errors surface.

User Story: As a user I open a file in a chosen app.
Expected Behavior: Open With… presents an app chooser under /Applications and opens with it.

User Story: As a user I reveal a file in Finder.
Expected Behavior: Reveal in Finder selects the file in Finder.

User Story: As a user I Quick Look a file from its context menu.
Expected Behavior: Context menu Quick Look should show a preview of the file.
Errors: Context-menu Quick Look calls preview.toggle but never openWindow(id:'preview'); the preview Window only opens via openWindow, so nothing appears. Also leaves preview.isVisible=true with no window, desyncing the CHANGES diff-peek toggle (next space is a no-op).
Fix: PENDING: call openWindow(id:'preview') after preview.toggle (mirror toggleDiffPeek).

User Story: As a user I create a new file.
Expected Behavior: New File… prompts a name and creates an empty file in the folder (or the file's parent); refreshes.

User Story: As a user I create a new folder.
Expected Behavior: New Folder… prompts a name and creates the directory; refreshes.

User Story: As a user I rename a file/folder.
Expected Behavior: Rename… prompts; refuses an existing destination name; otherwise moves and refreshes.

User Story: As a user I duplicate a file/folder.
Expected Behavior: Duplicate creates '<name> copy' (then ' copy 2', …) next to the original.

User Story: As a user I copy a file's absolute path.
Expected Behavior: Copy Path puts the absolute path on the pasteboard.

User Story: As a user I trash a file safely.
Expected Behavior: Move to Trash… confirms, then moves the item to the macOS Trash (never rm).

User Story: As a user I press space on a file to Quick Look it Finder-style.
Expected Behavior: Space on a FILES row opens the system QLPreviewPanel over all visible files, starting on the selection; its arrows page through.

User Story: As a user I press space on a change to see its diff.
Expected Behavior: Space on a CHANGES row opens a floating in-app diff window.

User Story: As a user I switch the diff between unified and side-by-side.
Expected Behavior: The peek header toggle switches layouts; the choice persists across sessions.

User Story: As a user I see +added/−removed totals for a diff.
Expected Behavior: Peek header shows +N green / −N red counts.

User Story: As a user I preview a text file's content read-only.
Expected Behavior: A non-changed text file resolves to in-app text content (else hands to Quick Look).
Errors: In-app read-only text preview is only reachable via the broken context-menu Quick Look (F4) or unreachable snapshot browse (G6) -> effectively unreachable.

Feature: Branch snapshot preview via git show
User Story: As a user previewing a browsed branch shows committed content, not disk (D2).
Expected Behavior: When browsing a snapshot, text preview comes from `git show ref:path`.
Errors: Branch-snapshot preview via git show is correct but only runs when isBrowsingSnapshot is true, which only branch browse (K1) sets — and K1 has no UI entry.
Fix: PENDING: depends on wiring K1 branch browse.

User Story: As a user I dismiss the peek with space or Esc.
Expected Behavior: Space or Esc closes the floating peek window.

User Story: As a user the diff view explains non-text diffs.
Expected Behavior: Binary files show 'Binary file'; empty hunks show 'No textual changes'.

User Story: As a user I keep teebe above other windows.
Expected Behavior: The pin toggles window level .floating/.normal and persists.

User Story: As a user I expand/collapse WORKTREES/CHANGES/FILES.
Expected Behavior: Each section header toggles open/closed with animation; headers always stay visible.

User Story: As a user the window resizes sensibly to the open sections.
Expected Behavior: All-closed -> compact height; only-WORKTREES -> content-sized & height-locked; CHANGES/FILES open -> free height.

User Story: As a user each repo reopens with the layout I left.
Expected Behavior: Open sections + window height are saved per repo and restored on select.

User Story: As a user the window chrome stays minimal.
Expected Behavior: Close/min/zoom buttons fade in only while the title bar is hovered.

User Story: As a user the window can't be dragged to an unusable size.
Expected Behavior: Min width 250; min height 150 when a scrollable section is open; height locked when content-sized.

User Story: As a user my manual window height is remembered.
Expected Behavior: Finishing a drag with a scrollable section open stores the new height per repo.

User Story: As a user my repos/prefs/selection survive relaunch.
Expected Behavior: Repos, floatOnTop, last selected repo+worktree, and per-repo layout are written to state.json.
Errors: persist() is never called on a selection change (only float/add/remove/setBase). addRepository also persists BEFORE selecting. Last selected repo/worktree is not durably saved.
Fix: PENDING: persist on selectRepo/selectWorktree; persist after add's await.

User Story: As a user teebe reopens where I left off.
Expected Behavior: Bootstrap restores repos, floatOnTop, and the last selected repo (then focuses primary worktree).
Errors: Consequence of I1: after a plain repo switch + quit, relaunch reopens a stale repo (the read side restores correctly but the saved value is stale).

User Story: As a user my last selected worktree is reselected, not reset to primary.
Expected Behavior: lastSelectedWorktreePath should reselect that worktree on launch.
Errors: lastSelectedWorktreePath is written but never read; bootstrap always focuses the primary worktree, so a non-primary last selection is lost.
Fix: PENDING: in bootstrap, reselect the worktree matching lastSelectedWorktreePath.

User Story: As a user a missing/corrupt state file doesn't break startup.
Expected Behavior: load() returns defaults when state.json is missing or unreadable.

User Story: As a user the tree/badges update as files change.
Expected Behavior: FSEvents on the worktree trigger a debounced status refresh + tree rebuild.

User Story: As a user recent writes mark a worktree live/busy.
Expected Behavior: Writes within the busy window flag the worktree live (dot) and busy (guard warning).
Errors: FSEvents callback records teebe's OWN writes (discard/trash/new/rename/duplicate) as worktree activity -> false 'an agent may be active' busy warning and false live dot for the user's own actions.
Fix: PENDING: suppress activity recording for teebe-originated writes (ignore window).

User Story: As a user I'm warned before mutating a busy worktree.
Expected Behavior: The confirm dialog adds a warning when the worktree was written within 5s.

User Story: As a user I view another branch's files without checking it out.
Expected Behavior: Selecting a branch loads its committed tree read-only (snapshot), working dir untouched.
Errors: Browse-branch read-only snapshot is implemented+tested in VM/core, but no UI invokes selector.browse(branch:) — no 'Branch ▾' selector exists.
Fix: PENDING: add a Branch menu that calls selector.browse(branch:) + stopBrowsing.

User Story: As a user I return from a snapshot to the live working tree.
Expected Behavior: Stop browsing reloads the worktree's own working tree.
Errors: stopBrowsing() is correct but has no caller (depends on K1 being reachable).
Fix: PENDING: add a 'viewing branch — return' affordance (with K1).

User Story: As a user I switch a worktree's branch with a safety confirm.
Expected Behavior: requestCheckout -> confirm -> `git checkout <branch>` with busy warning.
Errors: requestCheckout(branch:) has zero callers; the full checkout confirm path is wired but no UI sets a .checkout pending mutation.
Fix: PENDING: add a 'Checkout…' affordance that calls requestCheckout.

User Story: As a user branches are available for repo actions.
Expected Behavior: On repo select, local+remote branches are listed (used by Set Base Branch).

User Story: As a user I read a clear unified diff.
Expected Behavior: Old/new line numbers, +/−/space markers, color, long lines wrap to fill the window.

User Story: As a user I read a VSCode-style two-column diff.
Expected Behavior: Deletions/additions pair row-for-row; missing counterpart shows a faint filler band.

User Story: As a user hunks are labeled with their ranges.
Expected Behavior: Each hunk shows `@@ -a,b +c,d @@` plus any section context.
